PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul LaMartina for Bob Lalanne (Petitioner).

Mr. LaMartina stated that he is okay with his Client being appointed without IAEA (Independent Administration of Estates Act) authority, that he believes the Petitioner has done his due diligence in trying to locate the original will, and that the Petitioner checked the residence and the place of business, made inquiries with relatives, contacted the Decedent's Attorney's office and obtained a copy of the will. Mr. LaMartina also stated that the Decedent did not contact the drafting Attorney as far as making any changes. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required. Status on close of estate is set for March 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-22-2019 Continued To Aug-05-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul LaMartina for Robert Lalanne.

Mr. LaMartina addressed the issues raised by the Court stating that they conducted a search of the Decedent's residence and his principal place of business, that they made inquiries with relatives and friends, that they procured a copy of the will from the drafting Attorney Gerald Woods and that no one knows where the original will is. Mr. LaMartina also stated that there is no safe deposit box, that the drafting Attorney indicated that the Decedent never did another will, that the Decedent maintained relations with his natural Brother and Sister, and that he does not know if there was any conversation about what might or might not be in the will, and what is to take place. In addition, Mr. LaMartina stated that when the petition was originally done they did not petition for IAEA authority, that no IAEA authority was anticipated because there was only one account, that when the amended notice was prepared it inadvertently showed IAEA and that he has no qualms if it is not granted. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a supplemental declaration regarding due diligence.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
